member.name: M:Interop.AdeptCAC.NxProjectClass.GetRelTreeEx(System.Int32,System.String,System.Int32,System.Int32,System.Int32,System.Int32,System.Int32,System.Int32)
member.param: generally 0, 1 to process relationships that aren't required in order to open the parent
1 to not process children of an overlay
1 to not include a file and it's children if the file is in a filtered/suppressed library
member.summary: Build the relationship tree for a document record

member.name: M:Interop.AdeptCAC.NxProjectClass.GetRelTree3(System.Int32,System.String,System.Int32,System.Int32,System.Int32,System.Int32,System.Int32,System.Int32,System.Int32,System.Int32)
member.param: generally 0, 1 to process relationships that aren't required in order to open the parent
1 to not process children of an overlay
1 to not include a file and it's children if the file is in a filtered/suppressed library

ADEPTT_RTTREE_FILTER, filters items by relationship type generally RT_ANY for 'include children of any relationship type' If a relationship is found that is not included in the filter, then that branch is stopped. This means that that node's grandchildren will not be included even if they would have been included in the filter.

ADEPTT_XREF_LINKAGE_SEARCH, controls how the relationships are walked/seached to build the tree generally XREF_LINKAGE_CHILDREN for 'walk all children recursively' XREF_LINKAGE_DIRECTCHILDREN to get only the first generation children XREF_LINKAGE_PARENTS is forced when TreeStyle=RT_WHEREUSED XREF_LINKAGE_TRANSMITTAL continue walking transmittal children if the relationship subtype is 'include children'
member.summary: Build the relationship tree for a document record

member.name: M:Interop.AdeptCAC.NxTblClass.Seek(System.String)
member.param: [in] Text value to seek with. For the following Indexes, format the seek value as "1", or "2", etc.: S_RHFILE INBOXN NFMERR S_RHLIB S_RHUSER S_RHPATH For all other indexes... If seeking for partial matches, then "d" to find "door", "doorway", etc. If seeking for idential matches, in a character field, then "door ", left justified, space padded on the right to the width of the field, to find only "door". in a numeric field, then " 42", right justified, space padded on the left to the width of the field, fot find only "42".
member.summary: Seek for a record in an index by value. On success, the seeked record will be the current record in the table.
member.remark: Results: Character Field - values 987, 9876511111, 9876599999, 98765 "98765" , returns 0 , record = 98765 "98765 ", returns 0 , record = 98765 " 87654", returns -1, record = 987 Character Field - values 987, 9876511111, 9876599999, 9876555555 "98765" , returns 0 , record = 9876511111 "98765 ", returns -1, record = 9876511111 " 87654", returns -1, record = 987 Numeric Field - values 987, 9876511111, 9876599999, 98765 "98765" , returns 0 , record = 9876511111 "98765 ", returns -1, record = 9876511111 " 98765", returns 0 , record = 98765

member.name: M:Interop.AdeptCAC.NxTblClass.Skip(System.Int32)
member.param: [in] Number of records to skip, can be negative.
member.summary: Skip to the next record in the index. On success, the skipped record will be the current record in the table

member.param: [out] number of records skipped to get to the next unique value.
member.summary: Skip to the next unique value in the index current index.

If seeking for partial matches, then "d" to find "door", "doorway", etc. If seeking for idential matches, in a character field, then "door ", left justified, space padded on the right to the width of the field, to find only "door". in a numeric field, then " 42", right justified, space padded on the left to the width of the field, fot find only "42".
member.summary: Seek for a record in an index by value. On success, the seeked record will be the current record in the table.
member.remark: Results: Character Field - values 987, 9876511111, 9876599999, 98765 "98765" , returns 0 , record = 98765 "98765 ", returns 0 , record = 98765 " 87654", returns -1, record = 987 Character Field - values 987, 9876511111, 9876599999, 9876555555 "98765" , returns 0 , record = 9876511111 "98765 ", returns -1, record = 9876511111 " 87654", returns -1, record = 987 Numeric Field - values 987, 9876511111, 9876599999, 98765 "98765" , returns 0 , record = 9876511111 "98765 ", returns -1, record = 9876511111 " 98765", returns 0 , record = 98765

member.param: [in] Number of records to skip, can be negative.
member.summary: Skip to the next record in the index. On success, the skipped record will be the current record in the table

member.param: [out] number of records skipped to get to the next unique value.
member.summary: Skip to the next unique value in the index current index.

member.name: M:Interop.AdeptCAC.NxLoginClass.SendMail3(System.String,System.String,System.String,System.String,System.String,System.Int32,System.Int32,System.Int32)
member.param: [in] ; delimited list of FilePNEs to be attached, where a PNE is a Path, Name, and Extension. Attachments must be paths that can be seen from the Server. Or, if there is a single text file attachment, see AttachementIsSingleTextFile below. Or, to have the attachments sent to the Server, see bSendAttachmentsToServer below.
[in]Set to True if attachment is a single text file that can be read in and sent to the server if using SMTP. Otherwise, Attachments must be paths that can be seen from the Server.
[in] Set this to 1 to have the client create a single zip file of the attachments to be send to the Server. The Server will unzip the files locally to be included in the email as attachments.

member.name: M:Interop.AdeptCAC.NxLoginClass.CheckPassword(System.String)
member.summary: Check if this is the Current User's password.
member.remark: The current user does not have to be an Admin to use this function. After 3 consecutive calls with an invalid password ECLOGOUT will be returned. No further checking will occur, and ECLOGOUT will continue to be immediately returned if this call is attempted again. The user must logout and re-login to try again.
member.returns: ADEPT_ERROR_NUMBER EC_NO_ERROR if the password is correct ECBADUSER if the password is incorrect ECLOGOUT if checking has failed 3 times (see 'Notes' above).

member.name: M:Interop.AdeptCAC.INxLogin.CheckPassword(System.String)
member.summary: Check if this is the Current User's password.
member.remark: The current user does not have to be an Admin to use this function. After 3 consecutive calls with an invalid password ECLOGOUT will be returned. No further checking will occur, and ECLOGOUT will continue to be immediately returned if this call is attempted again. The user must logout and re-login to try again.
member.returns: ADEPT_ERROR_NUMBER EC_NO_ERROR if the password is correct ECBADUSER if the password is incorrect ECLOGOUT if checking has failed 3 times (see 'Notes' above).

member.name: M:Interop.AdeptCAC.INxLogin.SendMail3(System.String,System.String,System.String,System.String,System.String,System.Int32,System.Int32,System.Int32)
member.param: [in] ; delimited list of FilePNEs to be attached, where a PNE is a Path, Name, and Extension. Attachments must be paths that can be seen from the Server. Or, if there is a single text file attachment, see AttachementIsSingleTextFile below. Or, to have the attachments sent to the Server, see bSendAttachmentsToServer below.
[in]Set to True if attachment is a single text file that can be read in and sent to the server if using SMTP. Otherwise, Attachments must be paths that can be seen from the Server.
[in] Set this to 1 to have the client create a single zip file of the attachments to be send to the Server. The Server will unzip the files locally to be included in the email as attachments.

member.name: M:Interop.AdeptCAC.NxDomainClass.Login(System.String,System.String,System.Int32,System.Int32@,System.String@,System.Int32@,System.Int32@)
member.param: unencrypted value of S_PASSWORD in fm60usr
if the server says that I am already logged-in, login anyway
[out] if bSystemLocked, the message that was given by the admin
member.summary: Login to a Domain.
member.remark: ECRASHLOGIN - means that the last time the user was in, he crashed out. but, it is still a successful login if the system is locked, someone must unlock it from the Adept interface. if already logged in, call NxCore.NxCoreInterface.Error(ECUSERON, LoginName) and if it returns 1, call Login again with bLoginIfAlreadyLoggedIn = 1

member.name: M:Interop.AdeptCAC.NxDomainClass.LoginEx(System.String,System.String,System.Int32,System.Int32@,System.String@,System.Int32@,System.Int32@,System.String)
member.param: unencrypted value of S_PASSWORD in fm60usr
if the server says that I am already logged-in, login anyway
[out] if bSystemLocked, the message that was given by the admin
[in] selected culture during login, to be the active language
member.summary: Login to a Domain.
member.remark: ECRASHLOGIN - means that the last time the user was in, he crashed out. but, it is still a successful login if the system is locked, someone must unlock it from the Adept interface. if already logged in, call NxCore.NxCoreInterface.Error(ECUSERON, LoginName) and if it returns 1, call Login again with bLoginIfAlreadyLoggedIn = 1

member.name: M:Interop.AdeptCAC.NxCoreClass.GetLocalDisplayString_FromDateTimeIso8601(System.String)
member.summary: Converts a DateTime string in Iso8601 and returns localized DateTime.
member.remark: takes a DateTime string in Iso8601 in the form if local time, then YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS if utc time, then YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SSZ - where the Z is directy appended to the time returns localized DateTime string in the form "Edit/Options/Admin/Global User/Date Display Format" = ISO Format, then LocalTime in the form %Y/%m/%d %H:%M:%S "Edit/Options/Admin/Global User/Date Display Format" = Adept Standard Format, then LocalTime in the form %m/%d/%Y %H:%M:%S

member.name: M:Interop.AdeptCAC.NxCoreClass.GetLocalDateTimeIso8601_FromDateTimeIso8601(System.String)
member.summary: Converts a DateTime string in Iso8601 format to localized format.
member.remark: takes a DateTime string in Iso8601 in the form if local time, then YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS if utc time, then YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SSZ - where the Z is directy appended to the time returns localized DateTime string in the form %Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S

member.name: M:Interop.AdeptCAC.NxCoreClass.GetUtcDisplayString_FromDateTimeIso8601(System.String)
member.summary: Converts Iso8601 DateTime to UTC display string.
member.remark: takes a DateTime string in Iso8601 in the form if local time, then YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS if utc time, then YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SSZ - where the Z is directy appended to the time returns localized DateTime string in the form "Edit/Options/Admin/Global User/Date Display Format" = ISO Format, then UtcTime in the form %Y/%m/%d %H:%M:%S "Edit/Options/Admin/Global User/Date Display Format" = Adept Standard Format, then UtcTime in the form %m/%d/%Y %H:%M:%S
member.returns: String; DateTime as UTC Display string.

member.name: M:Interop.AdeptCAC.NxCoreClass.GetUtcDateTimeIso8601_FromDateTimeIso8601(System.String)
member.remark: takes a DateTime string in Iso8601 in the form if local time, then YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS if utc time, then YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SSZ - where the Z is directy appended to the time returns UTC DateTime string in the form %Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%SZ
member.returns: String; DateTime as UTC Display string in the form %Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%SZ.

member.name: M:Interop.AdeptCAC.NxCoreClass.SendSmtpMailEx(System.String,System.String,System.String,System.String,System.String,System.String,System.String,System.Int32)
member.param: [in] IP or alias of an SMTP Server, ex. relay.host.net or ES1

[in] ; delimited list of FilePNEs to be attached, where a PNE is a Path, Name, and Extension. Attachments must be paths that can be seen from the Server. Or, if there is a single text file attachment, see AttachementIsSingleTextFile below.

[in]If there is a single text file attachment, set this to 1 to have the client read the text file in to a string to be passed to the server. The server will write the string to file and make it an attachment.
member.summary: Send an Email message using SMTP
member.remark: SMTPServer ignored in 10.0 All SMTP goes through the Adept Server SMTP from Edit/Options/Admin/Configuration

member.name: M:Interop.AdeptCAC.NxCoreClass.GetLoginToken()
member.summary: Internal Use Only.

member.name: M:Interop.AdeptCAC.NxCoreClass.SendSmtpMail3(System.String,System.String,System.String,System.String,System.String,System.String,System.String,System.Int32,System.Int32)
member.param: [in] IP or alias of an SMTP Server, ex. relay.host.net or ES1

[in] ; delimited list of FilePNEs to be attached, where a PNE is a Path, Name, and Extension. Attachments must be paths that can be seen from the Server. Or, if there is a single text file attachment, see AttachementIsSingleTextFile below. Or, to have the attachments sent to the Server, see bSendAttachmentsToServer below.

[in]If there is a single text file attachment, set this to 1 to have the client read the text file in to a string to be passed to the server. The server will write the string to file and make it an attachment.
[in] Set this to 1 to have the client create a single zip file of the attachments to be send to the Server. The Server will unzip the files locally to be included in the email as attachments.
member.summary: Send an Email message using SMTP
member.remark: SMTPServer ignored in 10.0 All SMTP goes through the Adept Server SMTP from Edit/Options/Admin/Configuration

member.name: M:Interop.AdeptCAC.NxCoreClass.GetAppVersion()
member.summary: Gets the version number of this Application.
member.remark: 2 Digit - Main Release Version Ex. 10 for 10.0 1 Digit - Minor Release Version, 0 for 10.0, 1 for 10.1 1 Digit - Maintenence Version, 0 is pre-release, 1 is release, increments when existing function definitions change, are removed, or are reordered. 2 Digit - Maintanance Version, starts at 00, increments with fixes and compatible enhancements
member.returns: 6 digit version number, i.e.: 100100 as in 10.0.1.00
member.seealso: see application/on-line documentation for version information

member.summary: Gets the version number of this API.
member.remark: 2 Digit - Main Release Version Ex. 80 for 8.0 or 82 for 8.2 1 Digit - Compatibility Version, 0 is pre-release, 1 is release, increments when existing function definitions change, are removed, or are reordered. 2 Digit - Maintanance Version, starts at 00, increments with fixes and compatible enhancements

member.name: M:Interop.AdeptCAC.NxCoreClass.IsInitialized()
member.summary: Determine if the Core has been initialized.
member.remark: Should always be true if there is a Gui.

member.name: M:Interop.AdeptCAC.NxCoreClass.Initialize(System.Int32)
member.summary: Loads DLLs and opens the Error Message Database.
member.remark: This function is a requirement and must be called before many Core functions. It has already been called if connecting to through the Gui. This function loads DLLs and opens the Error Message Database. If this function does not return EC_NO_ERROR, you may not continue.

member.name: M:Interop.AdeptCAC.NxCoreClass.Uninitialize()
member.summary: Unloads DLLs, and closes the Error Message Database.
member.remark: This is done automatically when the Core goes out of scope, but may be called in order to re-initialize the core. Closes open database if there is one. Logs out of Current Domain if there is one.

member.name: M:Interop.AdeptCAC.NxCoreClass.GetErrorString(System.Int32,System.String@)
member.param: [out] The concatination of String1 through String4 seperated by NewLine.
member.summary: Retrieve the Error String from fmXXerr for an error number.
member.remark: Gets fields String1 through String4 from ADEPTT_DBNUM C_ERROR for this ADEPT_ERROR_CODE.

member.name: M:Interop.AdeptCAC.NxCoreClass.AdeptDateToStr(System.String,System.String@)
member.param: [in] A date formatted as an "Adept Date", i.e.: 20051231
[out] The return, formatted as a standard US Date: 12/31/2005
member.summary: Convert a AdeptDate (20051231) into a readable string (12/31/2005).

member.name: M:Interop.AdeptCAC.NxCoreClass.StrToAdeptDate(System.String,System.String@)
member.param: [out] The output as an Adept Date string. (YYYYMMDD)
member.summary: Convert a readable date string (ex. MM/DD/YYYY) via COleDateTime::ParseDateTime into an AdeptDate (YYYYMMDD).
member.remark: Valid input examples for the 'Str' parameter include: "12/31/2005", "31 December 2005", "December 31, 2005", "Dec. 31, 2005". The 'pAdeptDate' parameter would return "20051231" for the preceding examples.

member.remark: i.e.: "n:\test" to "\\Server1\Share1\test". If the path is already UNC it will return UNC.

member.summary: Converts a UNC path to a drive-letter based path.

member.remark: takes a DateTime string in Iso8601 in the form if local time, then YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS if utc time, then YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SSZ - where the Z is directy appended to the time returns localized DateTime string in the form "Edit/Options/Admin/Global User/Date Display Format" = ISO Format, then LocalTime in the form %Y/%m/%d %H:%M:%S "Edit/Options/Admin/Global User/Date Display Format" = Adept Standard Format, then LocalTime in the form %m/%d/%Y %H:%M:%S

member.name: M:Interop.AdeptCAC.INxCore.GetLocalDateTimeIso8601_FromDateTimeIso8601(System.String)
member.summary: Converts a DateTime string in Iso8601 format to localized format.
member.remark: takes a DateTime string in Iso8601 in the form if local time, then YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS if utc time, then YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SSZ - where the Z is directy appended to the time returns localized DateTime string in the form %Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S

member.name: M:Interop.AdeptCAC.INxCore.GetUtcDisplayString_FromDateTimeIso8601(System.String)
member.summary: Converts Iso8601 DateTime to UTC display string.
member.remark: takes a DateTime string in Iso8601 in the form if local time, then YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS if utc time, then YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SSZ - where the Z is directy appended to the time returns localized DateTime string in the form "Edit/Options/Admin/Global User/Date Display Format" = ISO Format, then UtcTime in the form %Y/%m/%d %H:%M:%S "Edit/Options/Admin/Global User/Date Display Format" = Adept Standard Format, then UtcTime in the form %m/%d/%Y %H:%M:%S
member.returns: String; DateTime as UTC Display string.

member.name: M:Interop.AdeptCAC.INxCore.GetUtcDateTimeIso8601_FromDateTimeIso8601(System.String)
member.remark: takes a DateTime string in Iso8601 in the form if local time, then YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS if utc time, then YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SSZ - where the Z is directy appended to the time returns UTC DateTime string in the form %Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%SZ
member.returns: String; DateTime as UTC Display string in the form %Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%SZ.

member.name: M:Interop.AdeptCAC.INxCore.SendSmtpMailEx(System.String,System.String,System.String,System.String,System.String,System.String,System.String,System.Int32)
member.param: [in] IP or alias of an SMTP Server, ex. relay.host.net or ES1

[in] ; delimited list of FilePNEs to be attached, where a PNE is a Path, Name, and Extension. Attachments must be paths that can be seen from the Server. Or, if there is a single text file attachment, see AttachementIsSingleTextFile below.

[in]If there is a single text file attachment, set this to 1 to have the client read the text file in to a string to be passed to the server. The server will write the string to file and make it an attachment.
member.summary: Send an Email message using SMTP
member.remark: SMTPServer ignored in 10.0 All SMTP goes through the Adept Server SMTP from Edit/Options/Admin/Configuration

member.name: M:Interop.AdeptCAC.INxCore.GetLoginToken()
member.summary: Internal Use Only.

member.name: M:Interop.AdeptCAC.INxCore.SendSmtpMail3(System.String,System.String,System.String,System.String,System.String,System.String,System.String,System.Int32,System.Int32)
member.param: [in] IP or alias of an SMTP Server, ex. relay.host.net or ES1

[in] ; delimited list of FilePNEs to be attached, where a PNE is a Path, Name, and Extension. Attachments must be paths that can be seen from the Server. Or, if there is a single text file attachment, see AttachementIsSingleTextFile below. Or, to have the attachments sent to the Server, see bSendAttachmentsToServer below.

[in]If there is a single text file attachment, set this to 1 to have the client read the text file in to a string to be passed to the server. The server will write the string to file and make it an attachment.
[in] Set this to 1 to have the client create a single zip file of the attachments to be send to the Server. The Server will unzip the files locally to be included in the email as attachments.
member.summary: Send an Email message using SMTP
member.remark: SMTPServer ignored in 10.0 All SMTP goes through the Adept Server SMTP from Edit/Options/Admin/Configuration

member.name: M:Interop.AdeptCAC.NxRecordClass.Fetch(System.Int32)
member.param: 1 to fetch the memos as well you may write to unfetched memos in order to do an update, but you may not read unfetched memos that you haven't written to.
member.summary: Fill the Record buffer from the server.
member.remark: Sufficient key fields must be set to make the fetch find a unique record. ex. C_USER - s_UserId ex. C_LIBSEL - s_LibId ex. C_TRUSTEE - s_groupid and s_libid

member.name: M:Interop.AdeptCAC.NxRecordClass.Append()
member.summary: Append the record to the table on the server.
member.remark: Sufficient key fields must be set to make the append not collide with primary key fields in the table.

member.name: M:Interop.AdeptCAC.NxRecordClass.Delete()
member.summary: Delete a record from a table.
member.remark: Only available on custom tables. To delete docrecords use NxCommandsSL.Delete To delete other records, use that item's manager class. ex. NxTLFDefManager to delete templates, launch applications, or filetypes

member.name: P:Interop.AdeptCAC.NxFldValClass.bBeenSet
member.summary: Has this field been set manually or by a fetch You can read fields that have been set even if there has not yet been a fetch or append

member.name: P:Interop.AdeptCAC.NxFldValClass.StringVal
member.summary: Get the string value of the specifed FieldName. Will convert non-string fields to strings for return. Or Set any Type of fields from a string, converting as necessary.
member.remark: Setting: FDT_CHARACTER - 1st character FDT_STRING - exact FDT_LOGICAL - "T", "Y", "t", "y", "1", 1 converted to 1 FDT_INTEGER - atoi FDT_DOUBLE - atof FDT_DATE - exact FDT_MEMO - exact Some records (ex.fm90Jobs and fm90Trnsm) will not allow any set of system fields, and require the records to be accessed (appended/fetched/updated) via their class (ex. NxJob and NxTransmittal), so check "LastError" after set.

member.remark: Some records (ex.fm90Jobs and fm90Trnsm) will not allow any set of system fields, and require the records to be accessed (appended/fetched/updated) via their class (ex. NxJob and NxTransmittal), so check "LastError" after set.

member.name: P:Interop.AdeptCAC.INxFldVal.bIsKey

member.name: P:Interop.AdeptCAC.INxFldVal.bBeenSet
member.summary: Has this field been set manually or by a fetch You can read fields that have been set even if there has not yet been a fetch or append

member.name: P:Interop.AdeptCAC.INxFldVal.StringVal
member.summary: Get the string value of the specifed FieldName. Will convert non-string fields to strings for return. Or Set any Type of fields from a string, converting as necessary.
member.remark: Setting: FDT_CHARACTER - 1st character FDT_STRING - exact FDT_LOGICAL - "T", "Y", "t", "y", "1", 1 converted to 1 FDT_INTEGER - atoi FDT_DOUBLE - atof FDT_DATE - exact FDT_MEMO - exact Some records (ex.fm90Jobs and fm90Trnsm) will not allow any set of system fields, and require the records to be accessed (appended/fetched/updated) via their class (ex. NxJob and NxTransmittal), so check "LastError" after set.

member.name: M:Interop.AdeptCAC.NxFileGuideItemClass.FindNodeNumber(System.Int32)
member.summary:
FindNodeNumber is fast because the whole tree is built at the beginning. It is easy to find 21 in the tree because less than 50, less than 30, so can skip 51 and up, etc. 10 -> 20 -> 21 -> 30 50 100 150
Walks the whole tree for JIT because node numbers are on-demand an not in order

member.name: M:Interop.AdeptCAC.INxDocRecord.GetInteger64Val(System.String)
member.summary: For an Integer64 Type Field specifically s_FileSize, return as a LONGLONG (or long for .NET)
member.remark: Some records (ex.fm90Jobs and fm90Trnsm) will not allow any set of system fields, and require the records to be accessed (appended/fetched/updated) via their class (ex. NxJob and NxTransmittal), so check "LastError" after set.

member.name: P:Interop.AdeptCAC.INxRelNode.bInfinite
member.summary: 1 if this node is Infinite, otherwise 0. If a.dwg xrefs b.dwg, and b.dwg xrefs a.dwg, then the tree would look like... a ---> b ---> a (infinite) Recursion stops when a node is detected as being infinite.

member.name: E:Interop.AdeptCAC.INxPlugInCoreEvents.DoesFileNeedExtraction(System.String)
member.param: Extension of the file in question.
member.summary: Return eTrue to request that the extraction continue on this file extension. The file will then be downloaded and extracted. Return eContinue if you are not interested in extracting this file extension. If noone requests extraction, the file will not be downloaded, and the extraction will be skipped.

member.returns: eTrue to reload the library card fields
